summary: Decompiled several item callbacks across Unown/Unknown, Butterfree/Patapata, Tingle/Tincle, and Goldeen/Oldottosea, with supporting item-var and attribute struct field exposure in itCommonItems.h. The PR body reports 100% objdiff matches and main.dol SHA1 OK; the decomp.dev bot confirmed +1216 matched bytes and 8 new 100% matches, while listing itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll as a 96.47% improvement in that report.
key_files: src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h - split padding in itTincle_ItemVars, itTincleAttributes, itPatapata_ItemVars, itPatapataAttributes, and itUnknownAttributes to expose fields used by decompiled item functions
src/melee/it/items/ittincle.c - decompiled it_802EBE5C, itTincle_UnkMotion7_Anim, it_802EC4D0, and it_802EC9E8
src/melee/it/items/itpatapata.h - changed it_802E15B0 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void(Item_GObj*)
src/melee/it/items/itunknown.h - changed it_802CE8D0 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void(Item_GObj*)
what_changed: Added typed implementations for item callbacks previously left as decompilation placeholders, covering Unknown/Unown initialization and blast-zone animation checks, Patapata animation toggling and spawning logic, Tincle state/timer/ECB logic, and Oldottosea collision handling.

itUnknownAttributes now exposes an HSD_Joint* x24[26] table; it_802CE710 uses HSD_Randi(26) to select a joint, initializes item vars and flags, calls it_80279CDC with attr->x0, and transitions via it_802CEC24.
itUnknown_UnkMotion0_Anim now includes gr/stage.h and checks Stage_GetBlastZoneRightOffset/LeftOffset/TopOffset/BottomOffset; if the item leaves the blast zone it calls it_802CE8D0 and returns false.

itPatapata_UnkMotion4_Anim uses a countdown in xDD4_itemVar.patapata.x28, reloads from itPatapataAttributes.x2C, toggles x44 with XOR 1, and sets or clears HSD JObj flag 0x10 on dynamic bones 18 and 11.

it_802E15B0 now derives a spawn position with lb_8000B1CC, signals the camera with Camera_80030E44, spawns another item through it_802DD7F0, copies knockback/angle/damage-direction and patapata.x4 into the new Nokonoko item vars, resets patapata.x4 to -1, and calls it_802DCE00.
Tincle item vars now expose x30, x40, x44, x48, and an itECB x68; Tincle attributes now expose x40 and s8 timer bounds x54/x55.
it_802EBE5C snapshots msid and vertical velocity into Tincle item vars, zeroes velocity, initializes a timer from attributes, stores the current y position, and calls Item_80268E5C(gobj, 4, 2).
itTincle_UnkMotion7_Anim decrements x20, transitions with Item_80268E5C(gobj, 7, 1) when x20 reaches zero and msid is 6, calls it_802EC35C if it_80272C6C fails, and returns false.
it_802EC4D0 computes an absolute range from Tincle attribute bytes x54/x55, assigns x20 = HSD_Randi(range) + x54, and enters state 10 via Item_80268E5C(gobj, 10, 2).
it_802EC9E8 copies the saved Tincle ECB, halves top/right/left for msid 6 through 11, and applies it through it_80275D5C without directly mutating the saved ECB.
itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll now branches on it_8026D8A4(gobj, it_802E3098), x velocity, and it_80276308 before calling it_802E3098 or it_802E2BC0, uses PAD_STACK(8), and returns it_802E35CC(gobj).
smart_moves: Exposed only the needed struct fields at documented offsets instead of broadly renaming or restructuring item data, keeping the decomp low-risk and offset-searchable.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes once concrete signatures were known, improving header usefulness for later callers without inventing semantic names.
Kept unknown field names such as x20, x44, x68, and attr x54/x55 where semantics were not proven, matching project convention for uncertain decomp fields.
Used a by-value local ECB in it_802EC9E8 before applying per-msid scaling, preserving the stored item-var ECB while matching the runtime adjustment behavior.

Matched item behavior through existing engine helpers rather than open-coding: HSD_Randi, HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll/ClearFlagsAll, Item_80268E5C, it_80275D5C, Stage_GetBlastZone* helpers, and spawn/camera helpers.
Added narrowly required includes such as gr/stage.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, cm/camera.h, baselib/jobj.h, baselib/random.h, and itnokonoko.h to support typed calls.
decomp_lessons: For item decompilation, xDD4_itemVar union layout is often the blocker; split padding with exact offset comments before trying to express behavior in C.

Article special-attribute structs may hide resource arrays or timer bounds; itUnknownAttributes.x24[26] and Tincle x54/x55 show why attributes should be typed as soon as indexed or arithmetic use appears.
Animation callbacks commonly return false while driving timers, state transitions, bone visibility, or offscreen despawn checks through item vars.
Item-spawn routines can require transferring parent combat state into child item vars, as seen with Patapata copying knockback, angle, incDamageDirection, and x4 into a spawned Nokonoko.
Collision callbacks can need apparently artificial PAD_STACK calls for codegen parity; preserve those when objdiff indicates a stack-shape mismatch.
Automated match reports and PR body verification can disagree or reflect different moments; record both rather than assuming every touched function was 100% matched.
naming_conventions: Item files use internal species/item names: itunknown for Unown, itpatapata for Butterfree, ittincle for Tingle, and itoldottosea for Goldeen.
Callbacks follow the existing UnkMotionN_Anim, UnkMotionN_Phys, and UnkMotionN_Coll naming pattern when behavior-specific names are not established.
Address-style symbols such as it_802CE710, it_802E15B0, it_802EBE5C, and it_802EC9E8 remain address-based when semantics are still uncertain.
Unknown struct fields remain offset-style names like x20, x40, x44, x54, x55, and x68, with comments preserving byte offsets.
Typed prototypes prefer concrete project types such as Item_GObj* over UNK_PARAMS once the function signature is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll and Tincle routines to preserve matching stack layout.
Preserved HSD_Randi call ordering for RNG-dependent behavior in Unknown joint selection, Unknown facing/random direction, and Tincle timer setup.
Used a temporary s32 toggle and XOR 1 pattern in itPatapata_UnkMotion4_Anim before writing back to x44, matching likely compiler output for the visibility toggle.
Kept exact float assignments and comparisons such as 0.0f velocity zeroing, +/-1.0F direction setup, and 0.5f ECB scaling.
Casted ip->facing_dir to s32 in the it_802DD7F0 spawn call, matching the typed call shape shown in the diff.
Represented bone visibility through HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll/HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll on dynamicBoneTable bones 18 and 11 rather than trying to abstract the flags.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp-dev bot reported matched code at 57.61%, +0.03% and +1216 bytes for GALE01.
decomp-dev bot listed 8 new 100% matches: it_802CE710, itPatapata_UnkMotion4_Anim, it_802EBE5C, it_802E15B0, it_802EC4D0, itTincle_UnkMotion7_Anim, it_802EC9E8, and itUnknown_UnkMotion0_Anim.
decomp-dev bot also listed itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll as an unmatched-item improvement from 0.00% to 96.47%, despite the PR body saying all functions matched via objdiff.
follow_up_queries: Check later history for whether itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll reached a decomp.dev 100% report after the bot's 96.47% listing.
Search for later PRs that rename Tincle item vars x20/x30/x40/x44/x48/x68 or attributes x54/x55 to semantic names.
Search for later PRs that rename Patapata x4/x28/x44 or attributes x28/x2C after more Butterfree/Nokonoko behavior is decompiled.
Look for other item spawn handoff patterns using it_802DD7F0, Camera_80030E44, and copied knockback/angle fields.
Audit Unknown/Unown data references for the HSD_Joint* x24[26] table to document whether it represents random model/joint variants.
